steps.title: Spot the map contrast
steps.content: Topographic maps show elevation above sea level, while bathymetric maps show elevation below the sea. The second sentence therefore needs a transition that marks direct contrast between two related map types.

steps.title: Rule out mismatched transitions
steps.content: Choice A signals a later event, but no time sequence is described. Choice B signals a conclusion, but the passage is not wrapping up an argument. Choice C introduces an example, but bathymetric maps are not an example of topographic maps.

steps.title: Choose the contrast marker
steps.content: The remaining transition signals opposition between the two map types on the same dimension: above sea level versus below the sea. The answer is D.
